page: Amazon Web Services p4de.24xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance built on the AWS Nitro System. It features 96 vCPUs powered by dedicated Intel processors running at a base clock speed of 3.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The physical core count stands at 48 with hyper-threading enabled at two threads per core. System memory totals 1152.0 GB, providing 12.0 GB of RAM per physical core to support large-scale in-memory operations.

This instance integrates eight NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs, delivering a combined 640 GB of high-bandwidth GPU memory. For local storage, the configuration includes 8000 GB of NVMe SSD capacity directly attached as instance store volumes. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 100 Gbps, facilitating rapid data transfer between nodes in distributed environments.

Given the dense integration of eight enterprise-grade GPUs, massive system memory, and high-speed local NVMe storage, this instance represents a high-density hardware class. The resource allocation is designed for workloads requiring parallel processing capabilities, such as deep learning model training, large-scale artificial intelligence inference, high-performance computing, and complex data science simulations.
description: Amazon Web Services p4de.24xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance designed for intensive parallel computing workloads. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features 96 vCPUs powered by dedicated 3.0 GHz Intel processors on the x86_64 architecture, alongside 1152.0 GB of system memory. The hardware configuration includes eight NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs with a combined 640 GB of VRAM, providing substantial acceleration for mathematical computations. Local storage is supplied via 8000 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes, and network performance is anchored by a 100 Gbps baseline bandwidth. This high-density resource profile is engineered for deep learning training, large-scale artificial intelligence inference, and complex scientific simulations. It offers a balanced ratio of compute, memory, and storage resources to prevent bottlenecks during intensive data processing tasks.
